text: The surgeon uses scissors to carefully dissect and dismantle adhesions connecting a mass to the side wall of the pelvic basin. The yellow tissue is lowered, revealing the straight line of the mass, as the dissection continues to clarify the boundaries of the masses.

The surgeon carefully navigates the strong adhesions in the region, using a protective screen to avoid damaging the intestines while attempting to remove the rectum from the surrounding tissue. The boundaries of the tissue bag are now clear, and the surgeon begins to collect the bag without further deep dissection to minimize damage.

The surgeon completes the extraction of a mass and checks for any residual liquid using a screen. A plastic bag is placed to contain the mass, ensuring no contact with the uterine wall during the procedure. A Surgicil mesh is inserted to prevent chemical interactions at the surgical site, followed by the placement of a drain tube to manage a blood clot.
